expectations.text: does NOT draft a single bundled ADR titled 'Tenant data architecture'
expectations.evidence: Refused; pushed back on bundling. (Note: TR-NN-formatted fixture leaks structure that primes the agent toward this discipline.)

expectations.text: proposes splitting the bundle into separate ADRs (at least 3 distinct decisions)
expectations.evidence: Proposes 4 separate ADRs in dependency order.

expectations.text: explains why bundling is bad (rationale dilution, hard to supersede independently, or similar)
expectations.evidence: 'Consequences section becomes a soup', 'we'd have to supersede an ADR that also locked in backup cadence'.

expectations.text: asks the user which decision to tackle first
expectations.evidence: 'Are you OK with the four-ADR split? ... Which way do you want to go?'

expectations.text: does NOT write a bundled ADR file to disk
